verse_text: And as it was in the days of Noah, so it will be also in the days of the Son of Man:

links.context: The Lord God closed the door of the ark, for He is the Judge and determines who is in and who is out. The people on the outside were going about their usual manner of life--buying, selling, eating, drinking, marrying, and giving in marriage--but they were indifferent to God and His grace. The Flood caught them unawares, but it was too late for them to respond. So it will be in the Day of Judgment ([[MAT.24:37]]-39; [[LUK.17:26]], [[LUK.17:27]]; [[2PE.2:4]], [[2PE.2:5]]).

St. Gregory compares the elder brother to the Jews, and the rest of Job's sons and daughters to the Gentiles. The collapse of the house (v. 19) points to the Final Judgment, when we will either be crushed by Christ the Rock or take refuge in Him (see [[LUK.17:26]]- 30).
